title: Efficient Subarray Sum Finder with Incorrect Handling of Negative Integers

statement.markdown: Write a function to find the maximum contiguous subarray sum of an array of integers. The function should handle arrays containing negative integers correctly.
statement.outputFormat: The maximum contiguous subarray sum, e.g., 7
statement.notes: The function should be efficient, i.e., have a time complexity of O(n), where n is the length of the input array.

constraints.description: The length of the input array

examples.explanation: The maximum contiguous subarray sum is 6 (subarray [4, -1, 2, 1])

hints.text: The Kadane's algorithm is a good approach to solve this problem.

editorial.title: Efficient Subarray Sum Finder
editorial.intuition: The key idea is to keep track of the maximum sum of a subarray ending at each position.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves trying all possible subarrays, which has a time complexity of O(n^3).
editorial.betterSolution: The Kadane's algorithm has a time complexity of O(n) and is suitable for this problem.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using dynamic programming and Kadane's algorithm.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proved by induction.

functionSignature.java: public int maxSubArraySum(int[] A, int n);
